name: MFA Fatigue / Push Bombing Detection
description: Detects and responds to MFA fatigue (push bombing) attacks where attackers repeatedly send MFA push notifications hoping the user will approve one out of frustration. Monitors for excessive MFA push requests to a single user, immediately locks the affected account, revokes active sessions, and notifies both the user and SOC. Includes AI analysis to assess whether the MFA bombardment is part of a broader credential compromise campaign.
author: T1 Agentics

required_integrations.name: Active Directory
required_integrations.reason: Lock account and invalidate sessions for MFA fatigue target

required_integrations.name: Slack
required_integrations.reason: Notify SOC and affected user about the MFA fatigue attack

data.title: MFA Fatigue Alert
data.summary: Triggers on excessive MFA push requests or push bombing alerts

data.title: Analyze MFA Push Volume
data.summary: Calculate push request frequency and identify the attack pattern

script.code:
def main(alert):
    push_count = alert.get('push_count', alert.get('mfa_attempts', 0))
    time_window = alert.get('time_window_minutes', 10)
    affected_user = alert.get('user', alert.get('target_user', alert.get('username', '')))
    source_ips = alert.get('source_ips', [])
    if isinstance(source_ips, str):
        source_ips = [ip.strip() for ip in source_ips.split(',')]
    was_approved = alert.get('mfa_approved', alert.get('push_accepted', False))
    return {
        'affected_user': affected_user,
        'push_count': push_count,
        'time_window_minutes': time_window,
        'pushes_per_minute': round(push_count / max(time_window, 1), 1),
        'source_ips': list(set(source_ips)),
        'was_approved': was_approved,
        'severity': 'critical' if was_approved else 'high'
    }

data.title: AI Credential Compromise Assessment
data.summary: Assess whether MFA fatigue is part of a broader credential compromise

config.custom_instructions: Analyze MFA fatigue attack context: determine if the attacker has valid credentials (which would be required to trigger MFA), check source IPs for impossible travel or known malicious infrastructure, assess whether any push was approved, and evaluate the risk of account compromise

data.title: Push Approved?
data.summary: Did the user approve any of the MFA pushes?

data.title: Lock Account Immediately
data.summary: Lock the compromised account — MFA push was approved by attacker

config.message: CRITICAL: Locking account {{$.nodes.code-1.result.affected_user}} — MFA push was APPROVED during fatigue attack. Account likely compromised.

data.title: Notify SOC — Account Compromised
data.summary: Critical alert to SOC — account compromised via MFA fatigue

config.message:
CRITICAL: MFA FATIGUE — ACCOUNT COMPROMISED

User: {{$.nodes.code-1.result.affected_user}}
MFA Push Approved: YES
Push Count: {{$.nodes.code-1.result.push_count}} in {{$.nodes.code-1.result.time_window_minutes}} minutes
Source IPs: {{$.nodes.code-1.result.source_ips}}

AI Assessment: {{$.nodes.analyze-1.result.summary}}

Actions Taken:
- Account LOCKED
- All sessions revoked
- All tokens invalidated

IMMEDIATE ACTION REQUIRED:
- Investigate attacker activity since MFA approval
- Check for data exfiltration
- Reset credentials before re-enabling account

config.summary: MFA fatigue attack succeeded — user approved push. Account locked, sessions revoked, SOC alerted for full investigation.

data.title: End — Attack Blocked
data.summary: MFA fatigue detected, no push approved, account temporarily locked

config.summary: MFA fatigue attack detected but no push was approved. Account temporarily locked, user and SOC notified. Credential reset recommended.
